---
title: "About"
toc: true
toc-location: left
lightbox: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{r setup}
source("_common.R")
```

Welcome! I am a climate researcher and R enthusiast. See my [CV](cv.qmd) for full details.

# Short Bio

Marc Lemus-Canovas is a Postdoctoral Researcher at [Andorra Research + Innovation](https://www.ari.ad/) (Andorra). His research focuses on **climate change and climate variability in the Pyrenees**, with particular emphasis on high-resolution climate projections for this mountain region. He holds a PhD in Geography (Climatology) from the University of Barcelona, where he studied extreme precipitation events, synoptic climatology, and climate teleconnections across the Iberian Peninsula and the Pyrenees.

# Research Interests

- Climate variability and change in mountain regions
- Extreme precipitation events and compound events
- Teleconnection patterns (WeMO, NAO, AMO…)
- Synoptic climatology and circulation weather types
- Urban climatology (Barcelona Metropolitan Area)
- High-resolution climate projections for the Pyrenees
- R programming and open-source tools for climate analysis

# The synoptReg R Package

As part of his doctoral work, Marc developed [**synoptReg**](https://lemuscanovas.github.io/synoptreg/), an R package for computing synoptic climate classifications and performing spatial regionalization of environmental data. The package is available on [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=synoptReg) and [GitHub](https://github.com/lemuscanovas/synoptReg).

# R Programming & Data Science

R is central to both Marc's research and his passion for open and reproducible science. He is enrolled in a **BSc in Applied Data Science** at the [Open University of Catalonia (UOC)](https://www.uoc.edu/), deepening his expertise in data analysis, machine learning, and statistical modelling.

This website serves as a place to share R tutorials and tips — mainly focused on climate data analysis, but also covering general data science workflows.

# Contact

Feel free to reach out via [email](mailto:marc.lemus@ari.ad) or through any of the social links at the bottom of this page.

`r last_updated()`
